zh.lifeSummary: 當James P. Massey於1823年出生在美國南卡羅來納州安德森時，他的父親Silas Massey47歲，母親Nancy Burris33歲。他於大約1845年與Frances Townsend在美國南卡羅來納州安德森結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1860年住在美國喬治亞哈特哈特韋爾且於1870年住在美國喬治亞哈特迪斯特利可特 1。他去世於大約1877年在美國喬治亞享年55歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当James P. Massey于1823年出生在美国南卡罗来纳州安德森时，他的父亲Silas Massey47岁，母亲Nancy Burris33岁。他于大约1845年与Frances Townsend在美国南卡罗来纳州安德森结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1860年住在美国乔治亚州哈特哈特韋爾且于1870年住在美国乔治亚州哈特迪斯特利可特 1。他去世于大约1877年在美国乔治亚州享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: The Crimes Act
historicEvents.description: The Crimes Act was made to provide a clearer punishment of certain crimes against the United States. Part of it includes: Changing the maximum sentence of imprisonment to be increased from seven to ten years and changing the maximum fine from $5,000 to $10,000.

historicEvents.title: Fort Sumter Constructed
historicEvents.description: In 1829 Fort Sumter is constructed in the Charleston Harbor, South Carolina. Fort Sumter is most known for being the site of the first shots of the Civil War. It is barely ready when the American Civil War starts.

historicEvents.title: Orders No. 25 Removes Cherokees
historicEvents.description: A small group of Cherokees from Georgia voluntarily migrated to the Indian Territory. The remaining Cherokees in Georgia resisted the mounting pressure to leave. In 1838, U.S. President Martin Van Buren ordered U.S. troops to remove the Cherokee Nation. The troops gathered the Cherokees and marched them and other Native Americans from North Carolina, Tennessee, and Alabama along what is now known as “The Trail of Tears.” Approximately 5,000 Cherokees died on their way to Indian Territory.
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.
Historical Boundaries: 1854: Hart, Georgia, United States
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: The First Civil Rights Act
historicEvents.description: The first federal law that defined what was citizenship and affirm that all citizens are equally protected by the law. Its main objective was to protect the civil rights of persons of African descent.

historicEvents.title: KKK Supression
historicEvents.description: In March of 1871, in an attempt to supress the Ku Klux Klan in South Carolina, President Grant sends troops in. Later that year in October, the KKK are told to disarm and break up. They do not do this and later many are arrested by the US marshals.

en.lifeSummary: When James P. Massey was born in 1823, in Anderson, South Carolina, United States, his father, Silas Massey, was 47 and his mother, Nancy Burris, was 33. He married Frances Townsend about 1845, in Anderson, South Carolina, United States. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in Hartwell, Hart, Georgia, United States in 1860 and District 1116, Hart, Georgia, United States in 1870. He died about 1877, in Georgia, United States, at the age of 55.

Il a épousé Frances Townsend environ 1845, à Anderson, Caroline du Sud, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il a vécu à Hartwell, Hart, Géorgie, États-Unis en 1860 et à District 1116, Hart, Géorgie, États-Unis en 1870. Il est décédé environ 1877, à Géorgie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 55 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: James P. Masseyが1823年にアメリカ合衆国サウスカロライナ州アンダーソンで生まれた時，父Silas Masseyは47歳，母Nancy Burrisは33歳であった。約1845年にアメリカ合衆国サウスカロライナ州アンダーソンでFrances Townsendと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1860年にはアメリカ合衆国ジョージア州ハートハートウエルに，1870年にはアメリカ合衆国ジョージア州ハートディストリクト 1に住んでいた。彼は約1877年にアメリカ合衆国ジョージア州で55歳で死去した。

ko.Mountain Creek Church, Anderson, South C: 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주 앤더슨 모우테인 크리크 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: James P. Massey(이)가 1823년 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주 앤더슨에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Silas Massey(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Nancy Burris(은)는 33세였다. 그는 대략 1845년 미국 사우스캐롤라이나 주 앤더슨에서 Frances Townsend(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1860년도에 미국 조지아 주 하트 하트웰에서, 1870년도에 미국 조지아 주 하트 디스트릭트 1에서 살았다. 그는 대략 1877년 미국 조지아 주에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Mountain Creek Church, Anderson, South C: маунтеин крик чурч, андирсон, Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Когда James P. Massey родился в 1823 г. в местности андирсон, Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Silas Massey было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Nancy Burris, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Frances Townsend около 1845 г. в местности андирсон, Южная Каролина, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности хартвелл, харт, Джорджия, Соединенные штаты в 1860 году и в местности дистрикт 1, харт, Джорджия, Соединенные штаты в 1870 году. Он умер около 1877 г. в местности Джорджия, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 55 лет.
